reasoning: We are coming off a sharp 26bp spike in the BB Option-Adjusted Spread from 1.56 to 1.82 at the end of Feb 2025, driven by a stock market selloff over tariff fears. In the short term, violent spread moves tend to partially retrace once the initial panic subsides. A statistical linear trend suggests an underlying rate closer to 1.74, so the probability of a further increase relative to 1.82 on the immediate horizon (March 9) is assessed below 50% (mean reversion downward). However, in the longer term, 1.82 remains very tight historically; before November 2024, the index spent most of the year consistently near or above 1.90-2.00. As time goes on, the chance of reverting to those long-term, structurally higher historical averages increases. Therefore, probabilities climb over time, crossing 50% for the late 2025 and 2026 resolution dates, while strictly adhering to increasing uncertainty and staying anchored closer to 0.5 for distant forecasts.
